notes: AC6 (T28) green: the S3 down() re-adds inventory_code nullable, backfills every row via concat_ws over coalesced split columns, then alter_column(nullable=False) to re-establish the original NOT NULL contract -- so a NULL insert is rejected after downgrade, exactly what not-null-constraint requires. The order is correct (backfill before NOT NULL, no constraint-violation window). Layering honored: the behavior lives entirely in the single S3 Alembic revision (repository/migration layer); the green diff touches only the migration plus test files (feature + step_defs), no route/service coupling. NFR-F6-S1-2 satisfied (quantity is never written or recomputed by the migration). NFR-F6-S1-4 satisfied: the T28 step defs assert against the real paired Lakebase branch (live connection, no mock or in-memory substitute). NFR-F6-S1-5 satisfied: DSN resolves from env (DB_NAME=stockflow), no hardcoded connection string in the revision. Naming/comments make the restore-the-contract intent clear and SRP is consistent with the AC1-AC5 conventions; no UI surface in this AC. No concrete rubric-cited improvement warranted.
